name: Monkeydude1313
description: The Monkeydude1313 method is a beginner-friendly Skewb solving approach. It works in three phases: solving the first layer, then the centers, and finally applying an algorithm for the last four corners (L4C). Its straightforward structure makes it an excellent entry point for Skewb speedsolving.

steps.name: Solve First Layer
steps.description: Solve one complete face and its adjacent centers, forming the first layer of the Skewb. This is done intuitively by placing corners one at a time on the bottom face.
tips: Start with whatever color is easiest to build
Place corners one at a time — each insertion is a simple sledgehammer move
Plan the first two corners during inspection

steps.name: Solve Centers
steps.description: Orient and position the remaining center pieces to match their adjacent solved corners. This step uses simple, short algorithms to cycle centers into place.
tips: Only a few center cases exist — learn them all
Try to solve centers without disrupting the first layer

steps.name: Last 4 Corners (L4C)
steps.description: Solve the last four corners on the top layer using a single algorithm or short sequence. Since the first layer and centers are complete, only the top corners remain.
tips: There are a limited number of L4C cases
Recognition is based on the colors visible on top-layer corners
Practice the algorithms until they are automatic

pros: Very easy to learn for beginners
Clear step-by-step structure with no ambiguity
Good foundation for learning more advanced Skewb methods
cons: Higher move count than intermediate methods like Sarah
Not competitive at the top level
First layer solving can be slow without practice
